steps.subgoalLabel: Round one number to a friendly number
steps.instruction: 29 is close to which friendly number?
steps.explanation: Friendly numbers (like 10, 20, 30, 100) are easy to add in your head.

steps.instruction: You rounded 29 up by 1, so you added 1 too many. Subtract 1.
steps.explanation: Always adjust back. If you rounded up, subtract the difference. If you rounded down, add the difference.
Compensation works best when a number is close to a multiple of 10 or 100.

steps.subgoalLabel: Break each number into tens and ones
steps.explanation: Breaking numbers into tens and ones makes them easier to add mentally.
Put the partial sums together. Splitting works well for any addition problem.
